faqs.q: What is neuroplasticity in simple terms?
faqs.a: It's the brain's ability to reorganize and adapt in response to experience, learning, and practice. The connections between neurons can strengthen, weaken, or rewire, so the brain keeps changing throughout life.

faqs.q: Can adults still rewire their brains?
faqs.a: Yes. Neuroplasticity isn't only for the young, and adults keep real capacity to learn and rewire, even if some changes come more slowly than in childhood.

faqs.q: How does neuroplasticity relate to therapy?
faqs.a: It's part of why therapy and skill practice can help. When someone practices new ways of thinking in CBT or faces fears gradually in exposure therapy, they're using the brain's capacity to learn, though meaningful change usually takes consistent effort over time.
